description: Make the goal compiler follow its own no-unsafe-zero discipline by clearing every non-interpreter [unsafe-zero] error from `goal check ./internal` (475 -> ~434, all remaining being the interpreter's Value union, left as a separate decision).

userStories.title: Set sum-type struct fields explicitly
userStories.description: As a goal maintainer, I need every Diagnostic and FuncSig literal to set its sum-type field explicitly, so the compiler's own source stops omitting an enum field whose zero is unsafe.
acceptanceCriteria: `goal check ./internal` emits no `[unsafe-zero]` diagnostic whose message contains `Severity Severity` (the 6 Diagnostic.Severity omissions are gone).
`goal check ./internal` emits no `[unsafe-zero]` diagnostic whose message contains `Mode Mode` (the 2 FuncSig.Mode omissions are gone).
Every `Diagnostic{...}` and `FuncSig{...}` composite literal under internal/ sets its Severity / Mode field (no reliance on the zero value).
userStories.notes: Diagnostic.Severity omissions include the empty `Diagnostic{}` sentinels in convert.goal and mustuse.goal; give each a real Severity or restructure the sentinel. FuncSig.Mode is in internal/sema. Editing .goal requires `task generate`.

userStories.title: Initialize sema.Info map fields
userStories.description: As a goal maintainer, I need sema.Info's map fields initialized at construction, so `Info{}`/`New()` no longer produces a struct carrying nil maps that panic on write.
acceptanceCriteria: `goal check ./internal` emits no `[unsafe-zero]` diagnostic located on an `Info{...}` literal (all 10 map-field omissions are gone).
`sema.New()` returns an *Info whose Enums, Sealed, SealedImpls, Structs, Methods, Interfaces, EmbeddedIfaces, FuncSignatures, ForeignMethods, and FromRegistry maps are non-nil.
Read-only callers of New() still behave (the full test suite in the gate stays green).
userStories.notes: New() at internal/sema/sema.goal returns &Info{} deliberately with nil maps for read-only callers; initialize the maps in the literal (or via make) since a nil map is a genuine unsafe zero under the safety-only rule.

userStories.title: Initialize backend emitter map fields
userStories.description: As a goal maintainer, I need the backend emitter's map fields set at construction, so the `emitter{...}` literal stops omitting nil maps.
acceptanceCriteria: `goal check ./internal` emits no `[unsafe-zero]` diagnostic located on an `emitter{...}` literal for a `map[...]` field (renames, armFields, taken, typeDecls, recvTypes, curParams).
The generated backend still transpiles the corpus identically (the corpus golden tests in the gate stay green).
userStories.notes: In internal/backend/emit.goal, emitFileWith constructs `emitter{...}` then assigns some maps after; move all map fields into the literal (e.g. `renames: map[string]string{}`).

userStories.title: Fix LSP struct unsafe-zero fields
userStories.description: As a goal maintainer, I need the LSP package's struct literals to set their unsafe-zero fields at construction, so internal/lsp is free of unsafe-zero omissions.
acceptanceCriteria: `goal check ./internal` emits no `[unsafe-zero]` diagnostic on any file under internal/lsp/ (roleVisitor.roles map and packageEntry.open pointer are resolved).
The LSP end-to-end tests in the gate stay green.
userStories.notes: roleVisitor.roles is a map[int]int (initialize it); packageEntry.open is a *openFile (make it Option[openFile] if genuinely optional, else set it explicitly).

userStories.title: Model optional pointer fields as Option
userStories.description: As a goal maintainer, I need genuinely-optional pointer fields modeled as Option[T], so an absent value is a safe None instead of an unsafe nil pointer.
acceptanceCriteria: `rpcResponse.Error` (and any other field that is nil in a valid state) is typed `Option[T]`, and `goal check ./internal` emits no `[unsafe-zero]` for those fields.
Every literal that omitted the field now type-checks (an omitted Option defaults to None), and consumers read it via `match` / `?`.
The `--json` / JSON-RPC wire is unchanged (Option[T] lowers to *T), verified by the LSP and cmd/goal tests in the gate.
userStories.notes: Pattern mirrors the completed Diagnostic.Fix -> Option[SuggestedFix] change (and relies on the statement-position Option-match backend fix already landed). rpcResponse.Error is in internal/lsp; a success response carries None. Only convert fields that are legitimately absent, not required ones (those are US-006).

userStories.title: Set required pointer fields explicitly
userStories.description: As a goal maintainer, I need every required (never-nil) pointer field set at its construction site, so no remaining non-interpreter struct literal omits an unsafe pointer field.
acceptanceCriteria: `goal check ./internal` emits no `[unsafe-zero]` diagnostic for plainFunc.fn, Package.Types, emitter.arity, or any other non-interpreter pointer field.
Each affected literal sets the pointer field explicitly at construction (no post-literal assignment relied upon for safety).
userStories.notes: plainFunc.fn (*ast.FuncDecl, internal/sema), Package.Types (*types.Package, internal/typecheck), emitter.arity (*arityResolver, internal/backend). These are always set immediately after construction today; move them into the literal.

userStories.title: Assert zero non-interp unsafe-zero and document interpreter
userStories.description: As a goal maintainer, I need the non-interpreter self-check to be clean and the remaining interpreter errors documented, so the scope boundary is explicit and enforced.
acceptanceCriteria: `goal check ./internal 2>&1 | grep '\[unsafe-zero\]' | grep -v '^internal/interp/'` produces no output (zero non-interpreter unsafe-zero errors).

A dated note in DECISIONS.md (and/or docs/STATUS.md) records that the remaining ~434 `[unsafe-zero]` errors are the interpreter's Value tagged-union, with the rationale for deferring the Value->enum refactor.
The full self-host gate (task check-full) is green with FIXPOINT OK.
userStories.notes: Capstone: US-001..006 do the work; this asserts the aggregate and records the interpreter boundary decided with the user (interpreter Value union out of scope for this PRD).
